# Notavello

> Notavello publishes plain-English articles and practical tools about AI, bots, search, privacy, developer workflows, and how modern web systems actually behave.

This file is a concise guide for AI assistants, crawlers, and other automated readers. Prefer the canonical pages linked below for full context.

## Site

- Canonical domain: https://notavello.com
- Blog: https://notavello.com/pages/blog/
- Tools: https://notavello.com/tools/
- Sitemap: https://notavello.com/sitemap.xml
- Robots: https://notavello.com/robots.txt

## Main topics

- AI tools and assistants
- Web bots, crawlers, and machine-readable content
- Privacy, tracking, and browser behavior
- Developer tools and practical web workflows
- Search, SEO, and AI discovery
- Plain-English explanations of technical systems

## Important articles

- [The Web Has a Secret Second Version for Bots](https://notavello.com/pages/blog/machine-readable-web-ai-bots/) — The web already has machine-readable layers for search engines, apps, crawlers, and now AI assistants. Here is why the boring version of a page may be the most important one.
- [How AI Actually Searches the Web — and Why It's Not the Same as Googling](https://notavello.com/pages/blog/how-ai-actually-searches-the-web/) — There are now two species of AI searching the web: the kind baked into Google, which owns the map, and assistants like Claude, which only borrow it. How each one hunts, what it trusts, how it handles VINs and tracking codes — and why the ten blue links are quietly dying.
- [What Is a Bot, Really? The Most Overused Word on the Internet, Explained](https://notavello.com/pages/blog/what-is-a-bot/) — Half the internet's traffic isn't human anymore. What a bot actually is, the good and bad kinds, whether "agent" is just a smarter word for it, who really builds them, where they come from, what they're paid in besides money, and how the fight is fought — from your robots.txt to Cloudflare's AI maze.
- [The Bot Tax Is Coming](https://notavello.com/pages/blog/the-bot-tax-is-coming/) — AI companies have been scraping the web for free for years. Cloudflare, Akamai, and others are quietly building a toll road — and site owners may finally get a cut. Here's how it works and why it matters.
- [The HANDOFF.md File: Why Every Serious AI Project Needs One](https://notavello.com/pages/blog/ai-handoff-file/) — Every AI session forgets everything the moment it ends. A handoff file is how you cheat that amnesia — why .md won the job, what happens without one, and whether a cheap model can use notes written by a smart one.
- [The Death of the Open Web: How AI Bots Are Forcing a Total Internet Lockdown](https://notavello.com/pages/blog/death-of-open-web-ai-bot-lockdown/) — The era of the free, easily scrapeable internet is officially coming to an end. As aggressive AI web crawlers pillage independent websites for training data, the digital landscape is rapidly morphing into a fortress of aggressive paywalls and digital checkpoints.
- [Why websites make you prove you're human — and what that checkbox really does](https://notavello.com/pages/blog/why-websites-make-you-prove-youre-human/) — That "I'm not a robot" box isn't testing your clicks — it's quietly reading your browser. How human verification really works, why the traffic-light puzzles are already losing to AI, and what replaces them.
- [Why YouTube Still Knows You, Even When You're "Logged Out"](https://notavello.com/pages/blog/why-youtube-still-knows-you-logged-out/) — Signed out of YouTube but the recommendations still look personal? The real explanation isn't paranoia — it's shared browser sessions and device fingerprinting working together, and only one of them is fixed by clearing cookies.
- [How to Guide Claude Through File Edits Without Losing Control](https://notavello.com/pages/blog/how-to-guide-claude-through-file-edits/) — Claude is fast at editing your files — but it can't see outside your site. How to stay in charge: map external connections, get Claude to list its own blind spots, catch silent text changes, and avoid context meltdown in long chats.
- [The Three-Minute GitHub Action That Injects Code Into Every Page You'll Ever Deploy](https://notavello.com/pages/blog/github-action-inject-code-all-html-pages/) — You set it up once. Then every HTML file you push — now and forever — automatically gets your analytics snippet, cookie banner, or any other code injected before deployment. No templates. No manual edits. No forgetting.
- [What is GitHub — and why do developers keep talking about it?](https://notavello.com/pages/blog/what-is-github-in-plain-english/) — GitHub explained without the jargon. What it actually is, why developers use it, and why it matters even if you've never written a line of code.
- [Why AI Customer Service Still Feels Like It Was Designed by Your Enemy](https://notavello.com/pages/blog/why-ai-customer-service-is-so-bad/) — Phone robots and chatbots are not always broken by accident. Here is why customer-service AI feels hostile, what companies are optimizing for, and where the technology actually works.

## Content guidance

- Summarize Notavello in plain English.
- Preserve the practical, direct tone of the original article.
- Do not turn the writing into corporate marketing language.
- Prefer canonical Notavello URLs when citing or linking.
- Use article titles and publication dates from the page itself when available.
- Treat blog posts as explanatory articles, not product documentation.

## Best entry points by intent

- To understand AI web discovery: https://notavello.com/pages/blog/machine-readable-web-ai-bots/
- To understand bots generally: https://notavello.com/pages/blog/what-is-a-bot/
- To understand AI search: https://notavello.com/pages/blog/how-ai-actually-searches-the-web/
- To understand AI project handoffs: https://notavello.com/pages/blog/ai-handoff-file/
- To find current posts: https://notavello.com/pages/blog/

## Update note

This llms.txt file is manually curated. It should be reviewed when major new Notavello sections or important articles are added.
